rulings.Case Title: EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOHN E. KING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 18, 2026, line 1. ASSIGNEE WOLVERINE ENDEAVORS VIII, LLC'S Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs. Assignee of Record Wolverine Endeavors VIII, LLC ("Wolverine")'s motion for attorney's fees and costs is continued for hearing to March 23, 2026. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA MAR VS. WESTERN SURETY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 18, 2026, line 4. DEFENDANTS RONY VELASQUEZ, ITU CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Motion To Set Aside Default. Defendants Rony Perez Velasquez and Itu Construction, Inc.'s motion to set aside default is granted. The court concludes that plaintiff's service of the statement of damages operated to amend the pleadings. The court finds Plotitsa v. Superior Court (1983) 140 Cal.App.3d 755, to be the better reasoned of the cases that are split as to the effect of service of a statement of damages. Plaintiff contends that the statements of damages were served as part of a judgment package in June 2025 but provides only the proof of service, which does not list the statement of damages, and in any event was not served in the manner of a summons. The court does not deemed the proposed answer filed but sets aside the default and orders defendants to file their answer by March 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: YOSEF PERETZ ET AL VS. PETER LOUKIANOFF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 18, 2026, line 10. DEFENDANT PETER LOUKIANOFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Peter Loukianoff moves to strike those portions of Yosef Peretz et al.'s First Amended Complaint that rely on Peretz's pleading of a contingency fee agreement that, the FAC alleges, was later orally modified. The motion is denied. Loukianoff asserts that the fee agreement, and its later oral modification, are unenforceable because Business and Professions Code 6147, subdivision (a), requires such contracts to be in writing, and imposes other requirements. A contract not drawn in conformity with the BPC requirements is voidable, not void. (Id., 6147, subd. (b); id., 6148, subd. (c).) The court cannot determine at the pleadings stage that the contracts were voidable or that Loukianoff has elected to void them. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SHAREEZ HAMM ET AL VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 18, 2026, line 14. PLAINTIFF SHAREEZ HAMM, ROBERT GRASSO, MAXWELL PICCARDO, MICHAEL VASQUEZ'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Off calendar. The complex litigation department designated this case complex and assigned it to department 304. Please re-set the matter in that department. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KALED MORSEY VS. LIBERTY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 18, 2026, line 15. PLAINTIFF KALED MORSEY'S Amended Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings. The unopposed motion to stay the case in its entirety is granted, without prejudice to a motion to lift the stay by any party who has not yet appeared in this case, and without prejudice to a motion to lift the stay based on changed circumstances. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
